crisped_perch_plate.name: Crisped Perch Plate
crisped_perch_plate.summary: A simple, filling plate that makes long walks and harder swings feel easier.

innkeepers_fishpie.name: Innkeeper's Fish Pie
innkeepers_fishpie.summary: A sturdy inn pie that leaves you feeling well-patched and harder to rattle.

riverlight_chowder.name: Riverlight Chowder
riverlight_chowder.summary: A rich chowder that steadies the nerves and wakes up the mind.

moonleaf_eel_skillet.name: Moonleaf Eel Skillet
moonleaf_eel_skillet.summary: A rich skillet supper that sharpens the head and leaves a cool herbal finish.
moonleaf_eel_skillet.unlock_text: You need the right kitchen note before you can turn eel and moonleaf into something worth serving.

dawnscale_supper.name: Dawnscale Supper
dawnscale_supper.summary: A careful rare-fish supper that rewards quick hands and cleaner finishing work.
dawnscale_supper.unlock_text: You need a proper supper note before trying to do anything this ambitious with dawnscale trout.

hedgeroot_hash.name: Hedgeroot Hash
hedgeroot_hash.summary: A hot skillet hash of chopped roots and hedge mushroom that sits well and keeps the legs honest.

wharfside_skewers.name: Wharfside Skewers
wharfside_skewers.summary: Smoky skewers that reward quick hands and lighter feet.

moonleaf_root_broth.name: Moonleaf Root Broth
moonleaf_root_broth.summary: A clear herb broth brewed from marsh root and moonleaf to steady the nerves and put warmth back in the hands.

lockfin_skillet.name: Lockfin Skillet
lockfin_skillet.summary: A hard-seared pike skillet with marsh root and black pepper that eats like cold water and holds like iron.
lockfin_skillet.unlock_text: You need a drowned-weir kitchen note before turning lock water pike into something worth serving.

loaner_pole.name: Loaner Pole
loaner_pole.summary: A town-owned pole with more repairs than pride, but enough patience for the Bramble River.

ashwood_rod.name: Ashwood Rod
ashwood_rod.summary: A cleaner ashwood rod that lets you hold the line with more authority.
ashwood_rod.unlock_text: Earn Brambleford's trust past the inn cellar before anyone hands you a better rod.

riversteel_rod.name: Riversteel Rod
riversteel_rod.summary: A serious river rod with a tempered spine and the manners of a tool built to land uglier things.
riversteel_rod.unlock_text: Prove yourself against ridge trouble before anyone trusts you with riversteel.

plain_hook.name: Plain Hook
plain_hook.summary: A simple baited hook that draws the wharf's ordinary river fish.

feather_jig.name: Feather Jig
feather_jig.summary: A bright-feathered lure that coaxes quicker, cleaner strikes from the better fish.
feather_jig.unlock_text: Work far enough into Blackfen before the good feather work starts circulating your way.

sinker_chain.name: Sinker Chain
sinker_chain.summary: A mean little weighted rig that keeps bait lower and drags stubborn bottom-feeders into bad decisions.
sinker_chain.unlock_text: Bring back a real Blackfen kill before anyone gives you the heavier chain rigs.

glass_spinner.name: Glass Spinner
glass_spinner.summary: A pale spinning lure that flashes like bad lampglass and tempts anything in the drowned line still making poor choices around light.
glass_spinner.unlock_text: Only anglers who've worked the drowned line get trusted with lampglass tackle.

brambleford_hobbyists_wharf.cast_text: You cast a line into the Bramble River and settle in to watch the surface.
brambleford_hobbyists_wharf.name: Hobbyist's Wharf

blackfen_approach_reedflats.cast_text: You pitch a line into the dark reed channels and watch the water knot around the stems.
blackfen_approach_reedflats.name: Reedflats

drowned_weir_drowned_causeway.cast_text: You cast into the black channel beside the drowned stones and watch dead lampglow crawl across the line.
drowned_weir_drowned_causeway.name: Drowned Causeway

field_bandage_roll.name: Field Bandage Roll
field_bandage_roll.success_text: You cut the silk down, bind in the herb packet, and finish |wField Bandage|n x2.
field_bandage_roll.summary: Turns silk and herbs into dependable wound wraps for the road.

ward_dust_packet.name: Ward Dust Packet
ward_dust_packet.success_text: You grind the dust finer, bind it in charm-thread, and finish a |wWard Dust|n packet.
ward_dust_packet.summary: A quick frontier ward packet meant to blunt the next ugly hit.

pitchfire_flask.name: Pitchfire Flask
pitchfire_flask.success_text: You bottle the resin, temper it with spiteful salts, and cork a |wFire Flask|n.
pitchfire_flask.summary: Bench-brewed fire for when the problem needs flames more than discussion.
pitchfire_flask.unlock_text: You need Veska's pitchfire pattern before you can bottle something this irresponsible on purpose.

focus_tonic_vial.name: Focus Tonic Vial
focus_tonic_vial.success_text: You thin the resin, steep the herb sharp, and cork a bitter |wFocus Tonic|n.
focus_tonic_vial.summary: A hard little bench tonic that clears the head and steadies the hands.

stitchleaf_salve_jar.name: Stitchleaf Salve Jar
stitchleaf_salve_jar.success_text: You warm the resin, work the mushroom down to paste, and seal a jar of |wStitchleaf Salve|n.
stitchleaf_salve_jar.summary: A resin-heavy field salve that closes the gap between a bandage and an actual night's rest.

purity_salts_satchel.name: Purity Salts Satchel
purity_salts_satchel.success_text: You dry the mix hard, bind it in paper, and finish a biting packet of |wPurity Salts|n.
purity_salts_satchel.summary: A harsh field-cleanse mix for poison, swamp filth, and other things that deserve an insult on the way out.
purity_salts_satchel.unlock_text: You need a proper formula before you can mix cleansing salts that won't do more harm than good.

latchwire_ward_bundle.name: Latchwire Ward Bundle
latchwire_ward_bundle.success_text: You bind the nails in ward-thread, hitch the charm knot tight, and finish a |wLatchwire Ward|n bundle.
latchwire_ward_bundle.summary: A quick roadward kit that throws a rough guard across the next ally likely to eat the hit.
latchwire_ward_bundle.unlock_text: You need Veska's latchwire notes before you can rig a guard bundle that won't come apart in the hand.

fenlight_talisman_setting.name: Fenlight Talisman Setting
fenlight_talisman_setting.success_text: You seat the drowned glass in a bright iron cage, bind it clean, and finish a |wFenlight Talisman|n.
fenlight_talisman_setting.summary: A finer bench charm built from weir salvage and steadied into something the marsh can no longer bully.
fenlight_talisman_setting.unlock_text: You need a proper talisman setting pattern before risking drowned glass in a piece meant to survive the road.

shift_outcomes.name: Counter Rhythm
shift_outcomes.text: You spend a while sorting boots by size, straightening cloak pegs, and handing out rope without letting anyone call it an adventure tax. Leda nods once. That counts as praise.

shift_outcomes.name: Sharp Eye
shift_outcomes.text: You help fit a farmer's boy for rain gear, catch a split seam before it becomes an argument, and steer two customers toward sturdier travel kit. Leda's pricing chalk looks friendlier after that.

shift_outcomes.name: Shopkeeper's Eye
shift_outcomes.text: You talk one anxious traveler out of the wrong boots, find the exact strap a scout came in for, and keep the front counter moving without losing track of a single order. Leda decides you can be trusted with margins.

ashwood_bow.text: He tightens the stave, resets the grip, and leaves you with a bow that answers quicker to intent.

bellguard_bastion.text: He plates the face in old lock iron, resets the grip, and mutters that the bulwark now has the manners of a gate that expects to hold.

bellkeeper_mail.text: He closes weak gaps, resets the shoulder, and leaves the mail looking less ceremonial and much more useful.

briarpath_raiment.text: He binds hide and reed cord through the raiment until the whole thing feels ready for wet ground, bad weather, and worse company.

chapel_blade.text: Torren trues the edge, sets a cleaner sun-mark into the steel, and hands back a blade that feels steadier than it has any right to.

cinderwire_staff.text: The new rod comes out of the forge hotter, cleaner, and a little too pleased with itself.

dawnbell_mace.text: He breaks down the old head, works in warding brass, and hands back a maul meant for uglier crowns than nails ever were.

emberglass_staff.text: Torren rewraps the haft in black wire and resets the glass so the whole staff hums hotter than before.

field_leathers.text: Torren reinforces the seams and adds quieter fittings meant for brush, rain, and fast exits.

grove_talisman.text: The charm comes back brighter, better bound, and much less interested in pretending it was ever decorative.

gutterfang_pair.text: Torren binds goblin chain into the spines, evens the pair, and tells you these are the sort of knives ambitious things deserve to meet.

hedgeweave_robes.text: The robes come back better weighted, better lined, and far less interested in catching on bramble or spark.

hookknife_pair.text: Torren files the hooks meaner, resets the grips, and warns you not to call them pretty just because they finally deserve respect.

ironroot_longblade.text: Tower steel and goblin chain go into the new edge, and the result feels less like a frontier blade than a final answer.

ironroot_recurve.text: Torren resets the limbs with ridge stock and cave leather until the bow snaps back like it owes you speed.

lantern_focus.text: The focus returns in a brass lantern cage, tighter to the hand and brighter to the will.

militia_blade.text: Torren strips the edge down, mutters about wasted iron, and sends the blade back better than it arrived.

mossweave_wraps.text: He reinforces the wraps with silk and thorn cord until they feel ready for weather, roots, and the sort of fast choices a trail demands.

nightpath_leathers.text: He tightens the seams, darkens the fittings, and leaves the leathers quieter than your excuses.

oakbound_shield.text: Fresh bands, a tougher face, and a better handgrip later, the shield feels built to answer uglier hits.

parrying_dagger.text: The dagger comes back slimmer, darker, and much more interested in ruining someone else's rhythm.

pilgrim_mace.text: A little rebalance, a little prayer-mark, and the mace leaves the forge with a steadier authority.

roadwarden_mail.text: Torren adds cleaner rings, tougher leather backing, and exactly one lecture about traveling light.

rootwood_staff.text: Torren hardens the grain with moonleaf resin and thornwood heart until the staff answers faster to intent.

sun_prayer_icon.text: He braces the icon in clean brass and somehow makes it feel both plainer and more dependable.

sunforged_blade.text: Drowned-line iron and hollow lensglass go into the new edge, and the result feels made for ending lights that learned the wrong lesson about staying lit.

thorncall_staff.text: Fen resin sinks into the staff grain and leaves it humming like dark water learning a tune it should not know.

trail_knife.text: The knife comes back leaner, meaner, and sharpened past the point where bad decisions ought to stop.

warded_kite.text: Fresh iron, cleaner rivets, and one quiet chalk mark later, the shield feels built for uglier nights than the last one.

wardens_cuirass.text: Torren works drowned-line salvage through the harness until it looks less like armor and more like a promise to keep standing.

wayfarer_vestments.text: New clasps, better lining, and a cleaner shoulder cut leave the vestments ready for longer roads.

wildbloom_talisman.text: The talisman comes back threaded in marsh feather and pale glass, softer in the hand and stronger in the answer.

drafting_table.name: The Drafting Table
drafting_table.summary: A blank builder's dimension waiting for the family's first custom rooms.

junkyard_planet.name: Junk-Yard Planet
junkyard_planet.summary: A scrapyard world of broken rails, humming salvage, and practical tech violence.

training_island.name: Training Island
training_island.summary: A fast bright world meant for ki, speed, and martial experiments.

blackreed_battle_standard.name: Blackreed Battle Standard
blackreed_battle_standard.placeholder: A wall hook waits for whatever proof finally comes down from the old watchtower.
blackreed_battle_standard.summary: The rain-dark standard Captain Varn flew over the tower, hung now as proof the ridge does not belong to him anymore.

hollow_lantern_prism.name: Hollow Lantern Prism
hollow_lantern_prism.placeholder: A black-brass frame waits for whatever finally comes back from the drowned weir beyond Blackfen.
hollow_lantern_prism.summary: The pale prism taken from the wrong south light at the drowned weir, hung now as proof Brambleford's first hard chapter ended with the lamp finally dark.

junkyard_beacon_core.name: Beacon Core
junkyard_beacon_core.placeholder: A brass label waits for the first relic from Junk-Yard Planet.
junkyard_beacon_core.summary: The first recovered heart-core from beyond the Nexus, still faintly warm with route light.

miretooth_fen_jaw.name: Miretooth's Fen Jaw
miretooth_fen_jaw.placeholder: A narrow iron cradle waits for whatever proof finally comes back from the Blackfen edge.
miretooth_fen_jaw.summary: The hooked lower jaw of the marsh predator stalking Blackfen, mounted now as proof the bog edge does not answer only to hunger.

potking_battered_lid.name: Pot-King's Battered Lid
potking_battered_lid.placeholder: An empty bracket waits for whatever ugly prize finally comes up from the goblin warrens.
potking_battered_lid.summary: The dented iron lid Grubnak treated like a crown, hung now as proof the warrens no longer rule the road below.
